articles.title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
articles.description:
From the Pops fireworks to a World Cup quarterfinal and Sail Boston’s Parade of Sail, Boston is about to cram its biggest summer moments into an eight-day stretch.

The post Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston hosts major events including the Pops fireworks and World Cup quarterfinal from July 3-10.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail will also take place, attracting thousands of visitors.
Residents should prepare for increased traffic and crowds during this busy eight-day period.

articles.title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
articles.description:
From Revolutionary War-era Boston to John F. Kennedy’s favorite booth, Union Oyster House has served oysters—and history—for 200 years.

The post Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Union Oyster House celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The restaurant has served iconic figures, including John F. Kennedy, in its historic booths.
Local residents can enjoy a piece of Boston's history while dining on traditional oysters.

articles.title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
articles.description:
From John F. Kennedy to Matt Damon, here’s a look at the famous guests who’ve dined at America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.

The post The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Union Oyster House, established in 1826, is America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
Notable guests include John F. Kennedy and Matt Damon, highlighting Boston's rich cultural history.
The restaurant's legacy attracts tourists, benefiting local businesses and preserving Boston's culinary heritage.
